metric_value: Founded May 1980 to design/manufacture/market supermini-class fault-tolerant computers for OLTP
OLTP applications in distributed/networked environments where high availability is paramount
By September 1982: domestic sales/support in Boston, Chicago, Dallas, Houston, Los Angeles, New York/New Jersey, San Francisco, Tampa, Washington DC
Multi-year agreement March 1982; Olivetti receives marketing rights in selected countries in Europe, South America, and Africa; shipments begin April 1982; expected to exceed $40,000,000 by 1985
High-availability TP market forecast: $6.4B (1981) → $27B (1986) — 4.2x in 5 years
Cowen/Datamation 1982/83 survey of 6,700 minicomputer buyers ranks hardware reliability as #1 selection factor
20-40 percent growth predicted for terminals, superminis, 32-bit minis in OEM and end-user CDP markets
Banking/securities/finance, manufacturing, services, government, distribution — all needing OLTP
Document dates to Kastner's Stratus employer era (1981-1985); reflects positioning that Kastner helped articulate as marketing/sales support staff
notes: Employer-era artifact per skill section 13.3 routing

metric_value: Founders averaged ~20 years experience; depth from HP, Honeywell, Data General gave team capacity for 'planned rapid growth'
John P. Morgridge (Stratus VP Marketing 1982) became CEO of Cisco Systems in 1988 — a major industry move that the 1982 document foreshadows by describing his marketing/planning capabilities
notes: Morgridge built Cisco from $5M to $1B+ revenue

metric_value: Stratus survived as independent vendor through 2014; later as Stratus Technologies under Penguin Solutions — among very few 1980s minicomputer-era companies still extant
